brief_justification: Correct answer (No) with valid causal reasoning about collider bias; Multi-level analysis identifying the collider structure, explaining why conditioning opens a spurious path, and contrasting with/without conditioning; Some self-monitoring via suppress/amplify framework but no explicit bias-checking beyond that; Basic verification by comparing conditioned vs unconditioned scenarios but no formal counterfactual test; Acknowledges the key assumption (no unmentioned causal paths) but doesn't quantify uncertainty; Systematically compares Method 1 vs no conditioning and explains why the alternative is superior; Full derivation from DAG structure through collider identification to conclusion, fully reproducible
